query_normalized: evaluating the robustness of llm-generated code to input perturbations computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on the adversarial robustness of standard deep learning models (e.g., CNNs) against input perturbations, whereas the user's question specifically targets the robustness of *LLM-generated code* (a software artifact) to input perturbations. This falls under the "Off-domain entirely" rejection rule, as the mechanism (neural architecture topology vs. code generation logic) and the domain (image/classification tasks vs. code synthesis) are distinct, despite sharing the general keywo
